mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million units signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 109 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 109 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 5% signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 250K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the CPI above 3% signals a strengthening inflationary pressure that reduces purchasing power and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 3% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real GDP below 1850 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 1850 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
